name: Green Pea Puree 1898
recipeIngredient: 4 cups shelled peas and their pods
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the peas in a saucepan and set aside.
2. Wash the pea pods and place them in a separate saucepan. Add water and bring to boil over medium heat, cooking for 15 minutes.
3. Discard the pods and reserve 2 cups of the cooking water.
4. Pour the reserved cooking water over the peas and bring to boil over medium heat, cooking for an additional 20 minutes. Drain the peas.
5. Mash the peas through a colander or food mill and place them in the top of a double boiler.
6. Bring milk to boil in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Add onion, bay leaf, parsley, and cloves, and simmer for 10 minutes. Strain the mixture and add it to the mashed peas.
7. In a saucepan, cook flour and butter over medium-low heat until hot and smooth. Gradually stir in milk and the pea mixture. Season with salt and white pepper to taste. Stir until the consistency is that of heavy cream, about 10 minutes.
8. Strain through a sieve.
[4 servings. Each serving: 161 calories; 170 mg sodium; 17 mg cholesterol; 6 grams fat; 20 grams carbohydrates; 9 grams protein; 3.75 grams fiber.]
